title: The Curious Case of the Chrono-Cog

start.text: You find yourself at the creaky gates of The Clockwork City, a place where time is allegedly a well-oiled machine, but more often than not, it's stuck in a loop of bureaucratic red tape. The smell of gears and oil hangs in the air like a forgotten promise.
start.image_prompt: A sprawling steampunk city with towering gears, clock towers, and a massive central clock, hazy atmosphere, intricate details, 8k resolution, by Jean-Baptiste Monge and James Gurney
start.environment: The city is a mess of whirring cogs, levers, and mysterious puffs of steam that seem to come from nowhere. Above you, an immense clock ticks ominously. Time feels... wrong.

city_square.text: The Clockwork City's Square is bustling with activity—or it would be, if time hadn't decided to take an extended coffee break. People are caught mid-stride, pigeons hang in the air like forgotten thoughts, and there's a definite whiff of existential dread.
city_square.image_prompt: A steampunk city square frozen in time, people mid-motion, birds hanging in the air, intricate details, 8k, by Peter Mohrbacher

npc.name: Ezekiel, the Tardy Timekeeper
npc.dialogue: 'Ah, you there!' A short, round man with an impressive mustache (and a slightly less impressive sense of urgency) calls to you. 'I could use a hand! Time's gone all... peculiar.'

chrono_explanation.text: Ezekiel explains that the city's heart, the Grand Chrono-Cog, has stopped turning. And without it, time itself is... well, a bit on the fritz. You’re starting to understand why breakfast took three hours this morning.

npc.name: Ezekiel
npc.dialogue: 'If you're brave enough (or daft enough), you could help fix it. Just head to the Cog Tower. But mind the Time Snakes.'

chrono_explanation.event: You realize that 'Time Snakes' is not a metaphor.

cog_tower_path.text: You make your way through the city streets, dodging frozen pedestrians and inexplicable gusts of wind. In the distance, Cog Tower looms like a stern headmaster who's just realized you've forgotten your homework.
cog_tower_path.image_prompt: A towering steampunk structure covered in gears and pipes, looming over a foggy city, high detail, 8k, by Dan Mumford

battle.message: Out of nowhere, slithering through the air, appear the Time Snakes—eels of pure temporal energy that have a habit of wrapping themselves around clocks... and unsuspecting adventurers.

time_snake_encounter.text: The Time Snakes hiss menacingly, their bodies shimmering like half-formed thoughts in the back of your mind. The only way to deal with them is quick thinking and quicker reflexes.
time_snake_encounter.image_prompt: Snakelike creatures made of shimmering light and energy, floating mid-air, high detail, dynamic lighting, 8k, by H.R. Giger

battle.message: Dodge the Time Snakes and make your way to the Cog Tower!

cog_tower_entrance.text: You reach the Cog Tower. It's even more imposing up close, like a particularly smug grandfather clock that knows more than it’s letting on. The entrance is a massive door covered in... more gears. Because of course it is.
cog_tower_entrance.image_prompt: A grand clocktower entrance with intricate gears and pipes, high detail, atmospheric lighting, 8k, by Alex Grey

npc.name: The Door
npc.dialogue: 'Password?' The door speaks. You really shouldn't be surprised at this point.

guess_password.text: You try several passwords. 'Time.' Nope. 'Eternity?' Not a chance. 'Tick-Tock?' The door laughs. Finally, after much trial and error, you try 'Banana.' The door swings open. Of course it does.
guess_password.event: You're starting to think the city's sense of humor is wearing thin.

inside_cog_tower.text: Inside, the tower is a mechanical marvel. Gears the size of houses turn slowly in the walls, and every few seconds a massive pendulum swings by, making you question your life choices.
inside_cog_tower.image_prompt: The interior of a massive clock tower, with gears turning in the walls and a pendulum swinging across the room, high detail, 8k, by Greg Rutkowski
inside_cog_tower.environment: The air smells like old metal and the faint, distant sound of ticking fills the space.

meet_the_clocksmith.text: In the center of the room stands an elderly woman tinkering with an oversized wrench and an expression that suggests she'd rather be anywhere but here. Her nameplate reads: 'Clocksmith Prudence.'
meet_the_clocksmith.image_prompt: An elderly woman in a steampunk workshop, surrounded by gears and tools, high detail, 8k, by James Gurney

npc.name: Prudence, the Clocksmith
npc.dialogue: 'You here to fix the Cog or just stand there gawking? Because I don’t have time for gawkers, and neither does the city.'

help_clocksmith.text: Prudence hands you a wrench that weighs about as much as a small horse and points to the massive Chrono-Cog. 'All you have to do is wind it back up. Don't let the Pendulum hit you in the process.'
help_clocksmith.image_prompt: A massive, intricate cog at the heart of a steampunk tower, glowing faintly, high detail, 8k, by Peter Mohrbacher

battle.message: The Pendulum swings wildly as you try to fix the Chrono-Cog. Timing is everything.

chrono_cog_fixed.text: With a final heave, the Chrono-Cog starts turning again, albeit with a sound that suggests it might need a bit of oil... and a vacation. But time begins to flow, and the city seems to exhale in relief.
chrono_cog_fixed.image_prompt: A massive gear starting to turn again, glowing with temporal energy, high detail, 8k, dynamic lighting, by John Harris

npc.name: Prudence
npc.dialogue: 'Not bad for an amateur. Now, get out of my tower before you break something else.'

return_to_square.text: Back in the city square, time has resumed its regular, monotonous march. Ezekiel greets you with a relieved grin, though you're pretty sure he didn't do any of the hard work.
return_to_square.image_prompt: A bustling steampunk city square with people moving once again, high detail, 8k, by James Gurney

npc.name: Ezekiel
npc.dialogue: 'Ah! Knew you could do it! The city's back to normal... more or less. Time's always a bit funny here.'

reward_offered.text: Ezekiel offers you a reward: a golden pocket watch, but you can't help noticing it doesn't actually tell the time. Seems fitting.
reward_offered.event: You've saved The Clockwork City, for now. Until the next time someone misplaces a cog.

finale.text: As you leave the city, you hear a faint ticking behind you. Time, it seems, has a sense of humor after all.
finale.image_prompt: A traveler walking away from a steampunk city under a twilight sky, high detail, 8k, by John Harris
